commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR resolves agent session keys in diagnostic transcript lookup, feeds transcript assistant context into session-attention classification, and adds regression tests for app-agent transcript diagnostics and recovery gating.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +168. Total +189 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible from the PR head: reuse an app-agent session whose transcript already has an assistant line, then start a new processing turn with no current progress; `Boolean(sessionContext.lastAssistant)` will still disable recovery. I did not execute it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the PR so transcript-backed classification only disables recovery when it proves current-turn progress.

target.title: fix(logging): resolve app-agent diagnostic transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes app-agent/OAuth stale-session diagnostics so `agent:<agentId>:<session>` keys resolve their transcript context, not only cron/run keys.
- Uses transcript-proven assistant progress to classify attention as a stalled agent run with `recovery=none`, instead of recoverable `stale_session_state`.
- Adds regression coverage for app-agent transcript lookup, attention classification, and heartbeat recovery gating.

Fixes #91505

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The diagnostic session context resolver only extracted `agentId` through cron-run session parsing, while app-agent/OAuth sessions use the canonical `agent:<agentId>:<session>` session-key contract. The attention classifier also only considered in-memory active work, so a local transcript that already contained assistant output could still fall through to the stale empty-session state path.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch reuses the canonical agent session-key parser at the diagnostic source boundary and passes the resolved transcript assistant context into `classifySessionAttention()`. That changes the state classification and recovery invariant at the source of the stale-session decision: transcript-proven assistant work is now a non-recoverable stalled agent run, not a recoverable stale session.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix for diagnostic transcript resolution, attention classification, and recovery eligibility.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the changed path is local diagnostic/session-state code, the proof exercises production TypeScript modules with a real JSONL transcript, and focused regressions cover resolver, classifier, logger, and heartbeat recovery behavior.
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused source/test pairing with no broad refactor, no dependency changes, no config changes, and no catch/default guard masking. The patch quality warning is acknowledged as session-state/security-boundary sensitive because it imports the canonical session-key parser and changes diagnostic recovery eligibility.
- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope and not changed: agent execution, provider routing, OAuth credentials, message delivery, session file format, schema, config/default values, migrations, public API behavior, and external channel behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the canonical session-key parser plus local session-state transcript lookup. This does not add a new public contract surface; it aligns diagnostics with the existing session-key contract. Compatibility/default behavior is unchanged for sessions without transcript assistant context. Related open PR scan / competition scan from the existing-PR flow found this PR as the active linked repair path, with no main/upstream coverage replacing this change.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 [label] `status: ⏳ waiting on author`:** Addressed by updating the implementation and proof rather than leaving the PR as log enrichment only.
- **RF-002 [P1] auto-closing linked issue while false classification/recovery remains unresolved:** Addressed by making transcript-proven app-agent work classify as `stalled_agent_run` with `recoveryEligible=false`.
- **RF-003 [P2] “Don’t close the stale-session bug with log enrichment only”:** Addressed by changing `classifySessionAttention()` and `logSessionAttention()` so transcript context affects classification and recovery, not just emitted log fields.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** App-agent/OAuth stale-session diagnostics now resolve real session transcripts and use assistant transcript context to avoid the false `stale_session_state` / `recovery=checking` path.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Without this, a session that already produced assistant work could be treated as a stale empty session and sent through recovery checks, producing misleading C4 diagnostics and risking the wrong recovery action.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules on Linux, run with `node --import tsx`, using a real JSONL state transcript under `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91600
node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91600-evidence/app-agent-session-diagnostic-classification-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Behavior addressed: app-agent/OAuth stale-session diagnostics use transcript context for classification and recovery eligibility.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules under node --import tsx with a real JSONL state transcript.
Exact path exercised: resolveCronSessionDiagnosticContext plus logSessionAttention from src/logging/diagnostic.ts.
session_key=agent:oauth-agent:main
session_id=oauth-session-1
session_file=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91600-evidence/app-agent-session-diagnostic-state/agents/oauth-agent/sessions/oauth-session-1.jsonl
resolver_fields=lastAssistant="OAuth agent listed 3 pending approvals."
classification=stalled_agent_run
reason=transcript_progress_observed
recoveryEligible=false
attention_log=stalled session: sessionId=oauth-session-1 sessionKey=agent:oauth-agent:main state=processing age=61s queueDepth=0 reason=transcript_progress_observed classification=stalled_agent_run lastAssistant="OAuth agent listed 3 pending approvals." recovery=none
ASSERT_PASS: transcript-proven app-agent work is classified as stalled_agent_run with recovery=none instead of stale_session_state with recovery=checking
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The resolver reads `lastAssistant` from the app-agent session file, attention classification returns `classification=stalled_agent_run`, `reason=transcript_progress_observed`, `recoveryEligible=false`, and the emitted log contains `recovery=none` instead of `recovery=checking`.
- **What was not tested:** No external OAuth provider, channel delivery, private CI, or live remote agent service was used; this proof is limited to the local diagnostic/session transcript path that the issue exercises.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`, and `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A local app-agent session with key `agent:oauth-agent:main` and active session file `agents/oauth-agent/sessions/oauth-session-1.jsonl` contains an assistant transcript entry, then stale-session attention is evaluated after the threshold.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The focused regression test exercises the resolver, classifier, logger, and heartbeat recovery boundary without requiring external OAuth credentials or a live channel. It verifies both positive behavior (`stalled_agent_run`, `recovery=none`) and the guardrail that `recoverStuckSession` is not called for transcript-proven assistant work.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts
# 1 file passed, 5 tests passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts
# 1 file passed, 11 tests passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
# 1 file passed, 70 tests passed

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check focused diagnostic files
# All matched files use the correct format.

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings focused diagnostic files
# passed

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state`, `merge-risk: 🚨 availability`, and `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary` were considered because this reads session transcript context and changes diagnostic recovery eligibility.
- **Risk explanation:** Session-state risk is limited to diagnostic classification of stale attention. Availability risk is limited to avoiding recovery for sessions whose transcript proves assistant progress. Security-boundary risk is limited to using the existing canonical session-key parser and local transcript path; no credentials, authorization checks, sandbox behavior, provider routing, or external channel delivery are changed.
- **Why acceptable:** The compatibility/default behavior remains the same for sessions without assistant transcript context, and transcript-backed sessions now match the issue’s source invariant: observed assistant work should not be treated as an empty stale session. The change is local, covered by focused regression tests, and backed by production-module proof using a real local transcript.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
